data.description:
## Why

`fsck` Check 3 (`src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py:148-171`) reclaims a stale git `index.lock` with the SAME time-of-check-to-time-of-use race that story df83 (sundried-bonny-sloth) just hardened in `gitutil._reclaim_if_stale_index_lock`: it reads `os.path.getmtime(lock_file)`, judges stale, then unconditionally `os.remove(lock_file)`. A peer can remove the stale lock and drop a fresh LIVE lock at the same path in that window, and fsck then unlinks the peer's live lock — corrupting the peer's index.

## What

Mirror the df83 hardening: re-validate device+inode+age immediately before the unlink; abort on inode-change or refreshed mtime. Consider extracting the now-hardened reclaim helper from `gitutil` and reusing it here so the two paths cannot drift again.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] fsck Check 3 re-checks file identity (device+inode) and age at the moment of removal; mismatch aborts the unlink.
- [ ] A deterministic inode-replacement-during-reclaim regression test covers the fsck path (or the shared helper it now calls).
- [ ] Existing fsck stale-lock behavior (stationary stale lock removed; young lock left) still passes.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
data.title: Harden fsck Check 3 stale index.lock reclaim against the same TOCTOU as df83
